differential_diagnoses: Iron-deficiency anemia due to chronic occult gastrointestinal blood loss
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
Pulmonary parenchymal or vascular disease related to historical GPA
next_steps: Complete blood count (CBC) to determine current hemoglobin level
Iron studies, ferritin, and reticulocyte count to evaluate for iron deficiency anemia
Repeat transthoracic echocardiography to assess aortic valve hemodynamics and cardiac function
B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) or NT-proBNP testing to evaluate for heart failure
Fecal immunochemical testing or endoscopic evaluation (EGD/colonoscopy) for occult gastrointestinal blood loss
Electrocardiogram (ECG) and potentially Holter or event monitoring to assess for chronotropic incompetence
Pulmonary function tests (PFTs) to further investigate the contribution of chronic lung disease to exertional dyspnea
